title: Protect Wireless Access Using Authentication and Encryption
description: protect wireless access using authentication and encryption

checks.name: Wireless Access is Protected Using Authentication
checks.purpose: Verify that all active wireless connections and saved profiles use WPA2 or WPA3 enterprise 802.1X EAP authentication and that open or PSK-based profiles are not present

checks.name: Wireless Access is Protected Using Encryption
checks.purpose: Verify that all active wireless connections use AES-CCMP or GCMP encryption and that WEP, TKIP, and other weak ciphers are not in use on any wireless profile

checks.name: Wireless Access is Protected Using Authentication
checks.purpose: Verify that wireless adapters are disabled on the server or if active use WPA2 or WPA3 enterprise 802.1X EAP authentication with no PSK or open profiles present

checks.name: Wireless Access is Protected Using Encryption
checks.purpose: Verify that any active wireless connection on the server uses AES-CCMP or GCMP encryption and that FIPS-compliant wireless encryption is enforced via Group Policy

checks.name: Wireless Access is Protected Using Authentication
checks.purpose: Verify that any active wireless interface uses WPA2 or WPA3 enterprise 802.1X EAP authentication via wpa_supplicant and that no open or PSK connections are active or saved

checks.name: Wireless Access is Protected Using Encryption
checks.purpose: Verify that any active wireless interface uses AES-CCMP or GCMP pairwise and group ciphers and that WEP and TKIP are explicitly absent from wpa_supplicant and NetworkManager configurations

checks.name: Wireless Access is Protected Using Authentication
checks.purpose: Verify that any active wireless interface uses WPA2 or WPA3 enterprise 802.1X EAP authentication via wpa_supplicant and that no open or PSK connections are active or saved

checks.name: Wireless Access is Protected Using Encryption
checks.purpose: Verify that any active wireless interface uses AES-CCMP or GCMP pairwise and group ciphers and that WEP and TKIP are explicitly absent from wpa_supplicant and NetworkManager configurations

remediation: Configure all wireless access points and client connections to use WPA3 or WPA2-Enterprise with 802.1X EAP authentication backed by a RADIUS server. Disable WEP and TKIP cipher suites on all access points and client configurations. Enforce AES-CCMP or GCMP as the only permitted pairwise and group cipher. On Windows, manage wireless profiles via Group Policy under Computer Configuration > Windows Settings > Security Settings > Wireless Network (IEEE 802.11) Policies and remove any profiles using PSK or open authentication. On Linux, update wpa_supplicant.conf to specify only enterprise authentication and strong cipher suites and remove any open or PSK network entries.
